UNDER CCP 401, PLAINTIFF PROPERLY COMMENCED THIS ACTION IN SAN FRANCISCO. THE APPLICATION TO HAVE THE MATTER TRANSFERRED BASED ON THE CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE AS PREMATURE. SUCH A MOTION CAN ONLY BE MADE AFTER THE DEFENDANTS ANSWER. SEE EASTON V. SUPERIOR CRT. (1970) 12 CAL.APP.3D 243, 245; BURAN EQUIPMENT CO., INC. V. SUP CRT. (1987) 190 CAL.APP.3D 1662, 1665. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Transfer Venue On The Grounds Of Improper Venue And Convenience Of The Witnesses Of Deft'S And Req. For Judicial Ntc
Off Calendar Aug-22-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS PRIMO NERY LARA, M.D., REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA and JACOB MCGEE SANDS, M.D.'S Motion To Transfer Venue On The Grounds Of Improper Venue And Convenience Of The Witnesses is Denied. Under CCP 401, Plaintiff properly commenced this action in San Francisco. The application to have the matter transferred based on the convenience of witnesses is denied without prejudice as premature. Such a motion can only be made after the defendants answer. See Easton v. Superior Crt. (1970) 12 Cal.App.3d 243, 245; Buran Equipment Co., Inc. v. Sup Crt. (1987) 190 Cal.App.3d 1662, 1665. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976

MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS PRIMO NERY LARA, M.D., REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA AND JACOB SANDS, M.D.' Motion To Transfer Venue Of The Grounds Of Improper Venue And Convenience Of The Witnesses Of Defendants. Denied without prejudice to being re-filed. Neither side provided courtesy copies of their papers as required by Local Rule 2.6B. Without courtesy copies the court is unable to review the parties' positions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
